dialogue.utterance: Hey sir, my name's Austin, I'm with the rescue squad.
What's going on today?
I just having like really, really sharp pain in this area.
Sharp pain?
Yeah.
When did that start?
Maybe like less than an hour ago.
Okay, has anything like this ever happened before?
Uh not not this bad. No.
Okay.
Hey, can I have you...
Yeah, you can just start right.
We're gonna put some stickers on your chest and try to get an idea of what's going on with your heart.
Do you have any medical history?
Uh it's hard to remember right now. I'm on some meds.
Okay, do you remember what any of them are?
Uh I'm taking uh Lisinopril.
Alright, do we have stickers?
We got stickers.
Like a statin. Uh, just blanking out, sorry, it's just really hard to
Yeah.
That way. Can you describe the pain for me? What's how does it how does it feel?
It's like a jabbing feeling.
Okay.
Yeah.
On a scale of 1 to 10, 10 being the most pain, one being nothing, where would you put it?
10? Okay.
Alright, does it go anywhere else? Is it or is it just in your chest?
It's just in my chest right now.
Just in your chest? Okay.
All right, so have have you um, did dispatch tell you to give some aspirin? To take some aspirin already?
Uh I don't have any in the house.

You don't? Okay. We got some. I'm gonna give you uh 324 milligrams of aspirin. That's gonna be four of these little orange pills. I want you to just take 'em here. Um you're gonna chew on 'em, all right?
Okay.
All right, just gonna [REDACTED] these here on your arm real quick.
Are you having any difficulty breathing?
Yeah, feeling a little bit like it's hard to.
Okay. Are you having any other complaints right now or is it just that chest pain?
Just the chest pain, sir.
Do you think you're able to pull your shirt up a little bit for me?
Yeah.
Just kind of hold it up here. Just gonna get some of these stickies on your chest.
Yeah.
Got one down here.
These are cold.
This pain won't go away, man.
Yeah.
Well, you did the right thing by calling 911.
Are we gonna go to the hospital?
We're just gonna work on this 12 lead real quick and then and then we're gonna load you in our ambulance and take you over.
Do you um, do you have a hospital you'd like to go to?
Uh [REDACTED].
[REDACTED]. All right, we'll go there. Do you have anyone that you want us to contact for you?
Uh, yeah, you can call my wife.
Call your wife? Okay.
Alright, just gonna feel where we're putting these stickers here real quick.
So what what were you doing when this pain started?
It's just like mowing my lawn, cutting the grass, and then all of a sudden it's just like out of nowhere. It's just like...
Man.
Has this ever happened before when you've been out mowing the lawn?
Not not really, no. It's just never I feel like sometimes it gets a little uncomfortable but it's never had this this much pain before.
Do you have any cardiac history? Any
I've I've been to the hospital a few times. I think they said I have like high blood pressure, some other issues and I wasn't paying attention when they told me.
Have you ever had a heart attack?
Uh no, I don't think so.
Am I having one now?
I don't know, we're gonna we're gonna
We're gonna find out here.
Yeah, we're gonna just take a look and then see what what we're doing is looking at the electrical activity and and we can from that determine um
Yeah.
if you're having a heart attack.
Alright, sorry.
Move this a little bit. Sorry. Move it a little bit.
Alright, 12 lead's good.
When was the last time you you ate?
Uh I had lunch around like four four hours ago maybe.
Good healthy lunch?
It was a pretty healthy lunch, had a sandwich.
Okay. Just the usual.
Alright, just keep holding that shirt up here for me and just try to stay as still as possible, okay?
Do we have a medic that can interpret this 12-lead?
I can interpret it for you.
Okay.
Alright, you don't have to stay super still anymore, it's all good. Just gonna print out here.
Feels like it's getting worse.
It's getting worse? Is it moving anywhere else or is it still just staying in your chest here?
Just jabbing, jabbing and then it's just going deeper into my heart.
Perfect timing, our medic's on scene now.
Hello gentlemen.
This is what we got.
What's happening to me?
Well, I don't think you're having a STEMI, honestly.
You've got ST elevation in all of the leads.
So I'm thinking you might actually have something called pericarditis.
All right? Still needs to go to the hospital for, but I don't think you're having a heart attack this time.
That's good.
Alright? That's good to know.
All right. Can we take you on down to the hospital?
Yeah.
All right, let's do it.
Alright, we're just gonna move you over to our stretcher, alright?
Yeah, just gonna stand and pivot, okay? Gotcha.
